user_message: Help me build executive brief for Fabrikam Residences. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 13:45 EST
Subject: URGENT: executive brief Issue at Fabrikam Residences

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with executive brief. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 17:18 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 12:47 EST
Subject: Re: Re: executive brief Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with executive brief. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:01:30] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into executive brief.

[00:05:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:22:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:25:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
